symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe pain and inconsolable crying on hip abduction / leg-opening

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute mechanical pain on abduction fits an unstable/dislocated dysplastic hip.
Severe pain on any hip movement in an infant is a cardinal feature of a septic joint and must be excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: New apparent left leg-length discrepancy (leg looks shorter)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Apparent shortening is the hallmark of a superiorly displaced dislocated femoral head (the carer-observed correlate of the Galeazzi sign).

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced/absent spontaneous movement of the left leg (pseudoparalysis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painful unstable hip is held still and used less.
Pseudoparalysis of a limb in an infant is a classic sign of septic arthritis / osteomyelitis.
A fractured limb is also splinted and not moved.

symptoms.symptom_name: Asymmetric / deep skin creases on the left buttock and thigh

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asymmetric gluteal/thigh creases are a recognised (non-specific) DDH screening sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Female sex and positive family history of infant hip problems

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Female sex and a first-degree family history are the strongest epidemiological risk factors for DDH.
Absence of reported trauma lowers but does not exclude fracture or inflicted injury in an infant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile on home thermometer; leg normal colour and warm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A single afebrile home reading and a normally perfused limb lower the probability but CANNOT exclude a septic joint in a young infant.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Barlow manoeuvre (posterior subluxation on provocation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Demonstrable hip instability on Barlow is diagnostic of a dislocatable hip — but is a clinician maneuver, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Ortolani manoeuvre (palpable clunk of reduction)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable Ortolani clunk reducing a dislocated hip is diagnostic and also mechanically argues against isolated infection — clinician-only sign, sealed.
A reproducible mechanical clunk implies instability rather than infection, but does not replace formal in-person exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive Galeazzi sign on examination (left knee lower with hips/knees flexed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Galeazzi positivity indicates femoral shortening/dislocation — a clinician-measured sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dynamic hip ultrasound (imaging of choice under ~6 months)

investigation_details.expected_result: Shallow acetabulum with subluxation/dislocation of the femoral head confirming DDH
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging the unstable hip is neither confirmed nor characterised, delaying bracing and risking permanent damage.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ultrasound is the confirmatory test; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute unstable dysplastic hip in an infant
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Developmental dysplasia of the hip with acute subluxation/dislocation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, acute movement-specific pain, apparent shortening, reduced limb use and the DDH risk profile point strongly to an acutely unstable dysplastic hip — but confirmation and exclusion of a septic joint require in-person assessment.

Severe pain on movement plus pseudoparalysis in a pre-verbal infant keeps septic arthritis live even when afebrile — the reason the correct disposition is urgent in-person assessment, not remote reassurance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person paediatric hip examination (instability testing)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Barlow/Ortolani/Galeazzi and abduction range confirm instability and help distinguish mechanical from infective causes — clinician-performed, same day.
Confirms and characterises the dysplastic/dislocated hip under ~6 months and guides bracing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (FBE, CRP, ESR) +/- joint aspiration if infection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required in person to exclude septic arthritis in an irritable infant limb; not obtainable via telehealth.
Considered in person if fracture is suspected or ultrasound is inconclusive; not first-line for DDH under ~6 months.
